title: First network node, second network node, first wireless device, and methods performed thereby for handling a carrier in a wireless communications network
abstract: A method performed by a first network node (111) operating in a wireless communications network (100) is described herein. The first network node (111) initiates (702) sending a first indication to a first wireless device (131) operating in the wireless communications network (100) using NB-IoT. The first indication indicates that a first carrier (141), the first carrier (141) being a standalone Narrow Band-Internet of Things, NB-IoT, carrier operating within a New Radio, NR, carrier, is: a) deployed in one of: a guardband mode and an inband mode, and b) shifted away by a frequency offset from a frequency channel raster grid used by the first wireless device (131).
claims:
Claims CLAIMS: 1. A method performed by a first network node (11 1) operating in a wireless communications network (100) the method comprising: - initiating (702) sending a first indication to a first wireless device (131) operating in the wireless communications network (100) using NB-loT, the first indication indicating that a first carrier (141), the first carrier (141) being a standalone Narrow Band-Internet of Things, NB-loT, carrier operating within a New Radio, NR, carrier, is: a) deployed in one of: a guardband mode and an inband mode, and b) shifted away by a frequency offset from a frequency channel raster grid used by the first wireless device (131). 2. The method according to claim 1 , wherein the initiating sending (701) is performed via one of: a) Master Information Block, MIB, b) Radio Resource Control, RRC, signaling sent on a NarrowBand Physical Broadcast Channel, NPBCH, and c) system information block, SIB. 3. The method according to any of claims 1-2, wherein the frequency channel raster grid is 100 kHz, and wherein the frequency offset is Â±2.5 kHz or Â±7.5 kHz. 4. The method according to claim 1 , wherein the first network node (1 11) operates in the wireless communications network (100) using NB-loT. 5. The method according to any of claims 1-4, the method further comprising: - initiating (703) sending a second indication to the first wireless device (131), the second indication indicating that the first carrier (141) is operating with a different Physical Cell Identifier, PCI, than an assumed LTE carrier, the first carrier (141) being an anchor NB-loT carrier. 6. The method according to any of claims 1-3, the method further comprising: - initiating (704) sending a third indication to the first wireless device (131), the third indication indicating that a second carrier (142) is 

 deployed in a guardband mode or in an inband mode, the second carrier (142) being a non-anchor NB-loT carrier, and - refraining (705) from sending a fourth indication to the first wireless device (131), the fourth indication being an information element comprising NB inband carrier information. 7. The method according to any of claims 1-6, the method further comprising: - configuring (701) the first carrier (141) to operate within the NR carrier. 8. A method performed by a second network node (112) operating in a wireless communications network (100), the method comprising: - initiating (802) sending a fifth indication to a second wireless device (132) operating in the wireless communications network (100), the second wireless device (132) being served by the second network node (112), the fifth indication indicating that a set of resources corresponding to a first carrier (141), the first carrier (141) being a standalone Narrow Band-Internet of Things, NB-loT, carrier operating within a New Radio, NR, carrier, are reserved. 9. The method according to claim 8, wherein the set of resources which are reserved further comprise a guard spectrum around the first carrier (141). 10. The method according to claim 9, wherein the guard spectrum comprises NB- loT subcarriers on a first subcarrier grid, the first subcarrier grid being different than a second subcarrier grid of NR. 11. The method according to any of claims 8-10, wherein the frequency channel raster grid is 100 kHz, and wherein the frequency offset is Â±2.5 kHz or Â±7.5 kHz. 12. The method according to claim 8, wherein the set of resources which are reserved further comprise a predetermined number of symbols, slots, and/or subframes. 13. The method according to any of claims 8-12, the method further comprising: 

 - configuring (801) a set of resources as reserved resources, the set of resources corresponding to the first carrier (141). 14. A method performed by a second wireless device (132) operating in a wireless communications network (100), the method comprising: - receiving (901) a fifth indication from a second network node (1 12) operating in the wireless communications network (100), the second wireless device (132) being served by the second network node (112), the fifth indication indicating that a set of resources corresponding to a first carrier (141), the first carrier (141) being a standalone Narrow Band- Internet of Things, NB-loT, carrier operating within a New Radio, NR, carrier, are reserved, and - rate matching (902) based on the received fifth indication. 15. The method according to claim 14, wherein the set of resources which are reserved further comprise a guard spectrum around the first carrier (141). 16. The method according to any of claims 14-15, wherein the guard spectrum comprises NB-loT subcarriers on a first subcarrier grid, the first subcarrier grid being different than a second subcarrier grid of NR. 17. The method according to any of claims 14-16, wherein the set of resources which are reserved further comprise a predetermined number of symbols, slots, and/or subframes. 18. A first network node (1 11) configured to operate in a wireless communications network (100), the first network node (1 11) being further configured to: - initiate sending a first indication to a first wireless device (131) configured to operate in the wireless communications network (100) using NB-loT, the first indication being further configured to indicate that a first carrier (141), the first carrier (141) being configured to be a standalone Narrow Band-Internet of Things, NB-loT, carrier operating within a New Radio, NR, carrier, is: 

 a) deployed in one of: a guardband mode and an inband mode, and b) shifted away by a frequency offset from a frequency channel raster grid configured to be used by the first wireless device (131). 19. The first network node (111) according to claim 18, wherein to initiate sending (701) is configured to be performed via one of: a) Master Information Block, MIB, b) Radio Resource Control, RRC, signaling sent on a NarrowBand Physical Broadcast Channel, NPBCH, and c) system information block, SIB. 20. The first network node (111) according to any of claims 18-19, wherein the frequency channel raster grid is configured to be 100 kHz, and wherein the frequency offset is configured to be Â±2.5 kHz or Â±7.5 kHz. 21. The first network node (111) according to claim 18, wherein the first network node (111) is configured to operate in the wireless communications network (100) using NB-loT. 22. The first network node (111) according to any of claims 18-21 , the first network node (111) being further configured to: - initiate (703) sending a second indication to the first wireless device (131), the second indication being configured to indicate that the first carrier (141) is operating with a different Physical Cell Identifier, PCI, than an assumed LTE carrier, the first carrier (141) being an anchor NB- loT carrier. 23. The first network node (111) according to any of claims 18-21 , the first network node (111) being further configured to: - initiate (704) sending a third indication to the first wireless device (131), the third indication being configured to indicate that a second carrier (142) is deployed in a guardband mode or in an inband mode, the second carrier (142) being a non-anchor NB-loT carrier, and 

 - refrain (705) from sending a fourth indication to the first wireless device (131), the fourth indication being configured to be an information element comprising NB inband carrier information. 24. The first network node (111) according to any of claims 18-23, the first network node (111) being further configured to: - configure (701) the first carrier (141) to operate within the NR carrier. 25. A second network node (112) configured to operate in a wireless communications network (100), the second network node (112) being further configured to: - initiate (802) sending a fifth indication to a second wireless device (132) configured to operate in the wireless communications network (100), the second wireless device (132) being configured to be served by the second network node (112), the fifth indication being configured to indicate that a set of resources corresponding to a first carrier (141), the first carrier (141) being configured to be a standalone Narrow Band- Internet of Things, NB-loT, carrier operating within a New Radio, NR, carrier, are reserved. 26. The second network node (112) according to claim 25, wherein the set of resources which are configured to be reserved are further configured to comprise a guard spectrum around the first carrier (141). 27. The second network node (112) according to claim 26, wherein the guard spectrum is configured to comprise NB-loT subcarriers on a first subcarrier grid, the first subcarrier grid being configured to be different than a second subcarrier grid of NR. 28. The second network node (112) according to any of claims 25-27, wherein the frequency channel raster grid is configured to be 100 kHz, and wherein the frequency offset is Â±2.5 kHz or Â±7.5 kHz. 29. The second network node (112) according to claim 25, wherein the set of resources which are configured to be reserved are further configured to comprise a predetermined number of symbols, slots, and/or subframes. 30. The second network node (112) according to any of claims 25-29, the second network node (112) being further configured to: - configure (801) a set of resources as reserved resources, the set of resources being configured to correspond to the first carrier (141). 31. A second wireless device (132) configured to operate in a wireless communications network (100), the second wireless device (132) being further configured to: - receive (901) a fifth indication from a second network node (112) configured to operate in the wireless communications network (100), the second wireless device (132) being configured to be served by the second network node (112), the fifth indication being configured to indicate that a set of resources corresponding to a first carrier (141), the first carrier (141) being configured to be a standalone Narrow Band- Internet of Things, NB-loT, carrier operating within a New Radio, NR, carrier, are reserved, and - rate match (902) based on the fifth indication configured to be received. 32. The second wireless device (132) according to claim 31 , wherein the set of resources which are configured to be reserved are further configured to comprise a guard spectrum around the first carrier (141). 33. The second wireless device (132) according to any of claims 31-32, wherein the guard spectrum is configured to comprise NB-loT subcarriers on a first subcarrier grid, the first subcarrier grid being different than a second subcarrier grid of NR. 34. The second wireless device (132) according to any of claims 31-33, wherein the set of resources which are configured to be reserved are further configured to comprise a predetermined number of symbols, slots, and/or subframes.
